REIMAGINING
INSURANCE.

A retrospective redesign that reframes a digitized sales tool as a guided, human-centered financial consultation experience — and one designer's evolution from UI to Product.

01
01 Overview

The Origin Story

2021
Original Project Year
5+
Consultation Flow Steps
iPad
Target Platform

In 2021, I worked on Prudential Vietnam's Point of Sale (POS) platform, an iPad-based solution designed to support insurance advisors during customer consultations and policy sign-ups.

At the time, my role was primarily focused on UI execution based on requirements from Business Analysts and stakeholders.

Looking back at the project through a 2026 Product Design perspective, I realized this was never just a workflow digitization problem.

At its core, the product was solving deeper challenges around building trust in complex financial decisions, reducing anxiety during consultations, supporting emotionally driven decision-making, and transforming insurance consultations into more human-centered experiences.

Rather than viewing POS as a "sales tool," this retrospective redesign reframes the product as: a guided digital financial consultation experience.

🛡️
Building Trust
In complex financial decisions
💙
Reducing Anxiety
During consultations
🧭
Decision Support
Emotionally driven decision-making
✨
Human-Centered
Transforming consultation experiences
02
02 Problem Space

The Friction Points

In 2021, insurance consultations in Vietnam still relied heavily on printed brochures, complex paperwork, manual calculations, and fragmented operational workflows. This created friction for businesses, advisors, and customers alike.

🏢
Business Challenges
  • Inconsistent consultation experiences between advisors
  • Paper-heavy workflows slowing operations
  • Long consultation flows reducing conversion opportunities
  • Difficulty building trust around complex financial products
👥
Advisor Challenges
  • Difficulty explaining abstract insurance concepts
  • High cognitive load when presenting multiple product packages
  • Limited ability to create visual financial storytelling during consultations
💭
Customer Challenges
  • Financial information overload
  • Difficulty understanding long-term value
  • Anxiety when making insurance decisions

Looking back now, I realize that behind every screen existed far more than UI alone: compliance workflows, behavioral psychology, trust-building mechanisms, and operational complexity.

03
03 Role & Product Reframing

From Executor
to Architect

In the original 2021 project, I worked as a Junior UI/UX Designer responsible for designing iPad interfaces, building consultation layouts, improving readability and usability, and supporting visual consistency across the platform. The 2026 retrospective redesign allowed me to revisit the product through a more mature Product Design lens.

2021
Original Role
Digitized Sales Support Tool

The platform originally functioned primarily as a digitized sales support tool. Its main goal was to digitize traditional insurance consultation workflows.

  • Designing iPad interfaces
  • Building consultation layouts
  • Improving readability and usability
  • Supporting visual consistency
→
2026
Retrospective Vision
AI-Assisted Financial Consultation Experience

If redesigned today, the platform would be repositioned as an AI-assisted financial consultation experience focused on personalized guidance, trust-building, and decision support.

  • Personalized financial guidance
  • Trust-building mechanisms
  • Financial storytelling
  • Decision support systems

In 2021, the experience felt like...

"filling out insurance forms"

In 2026, it should feel like...

"planning and protecting a family's future."

04
04 Design Principles

The Guiding North Stars

01
Principle 01

Trust-First UX

Insurance products are fundamentally built on trust. The experience prioritizes clarity, transparency, emotional comfort, and reassurance — making every interaction feel safe and human.

Emotional Design
02
Principle 02

Financial Storytelling

Instead of presenting raw financial data, the platform transforms projections into relatable life narratives around future goals, family protection, and long-term financial planning.

Narrative UX
03
Principle 03

Progressive Disclosure

Not every user requires the same level of detail. Optional deep-dive screens allow users to explore additional information without overwhelming the primary consultation flow.

Information Architecture
04
Principle 04

Assisted Experience Design

The product is designed for guided consultation environments. The interface supports conversations between advisors and customers rather than functioning as a self-service application.

Conversation Design
05
05 Consultation Flow

The Journey,
Screen by Screen

Each step in the consultation flow is carefully designed to reduce friction, build trust, and guide customers toward confident financial decisions.

Welcome · Step 0

Welcome Consultation

Goal
Reduce customer anxiety before the consultation begins by introducing the consultation process, estimated duration, privacy commitments, and consultation expectations.
UX Insight: Customers are not simply onboarding into an application. They are entering an emotionally significant financial conversation.
Step 1A · Customer Snapshot

Customer Snapshot

Goal
Collect financial background and personal context to create personalized recommendations.
Key Interactions
  • Entering personal information
  • Setting up financial profiles
  • Selecting family status
Step 2A · AI Recommendation

AI Recommendation

Goal
Recommend suitable insurance solutions based on customer needs — with suitability scores and smart product matching.
Key Interactions
  • Solution cards with product comparisons
  • Suitability scores (90%, 75%, 60%)
  • Recommendation groups by goal
UX Goal
Reduce decision fatigue and simplify product discovery.
Step 2B · Optional Deep Dive

Product Detail

Goal
Allow customers to explore deeper product information including benefits, coverage scope, and recommendation reasoning.
UX Goal
Increase trust before entering financial projection scenarios.
Step 3A · Financial Projection

Financial Projection

Goal
Visualize long-term financial outcomes with interactive, adjustable projections.
Key Interactions
  • Adjusting premium amounts via slider
  • Changing contract durations
  • Interacting with dynamic charts
UX Goal
Transform abstract financial concepts into understandable visual outcomes.
Step 3B · Optional Scenario

Scenario Simulation

Goal
Help users understand the impact of life uncertainties such as critical illness, unemployment, children's education planning, and early retirement.
UX Insight: People often react more emotionally to potential loss than future gain. This screen introduces emotional visualization into financial decision-making.
Step 4A · Benefits & Transparency

Benefits &
Transparency

Goal
Clearly explain policy benefits, coverage scope, exclusions, and payout scenarios with full transparency.
UX Goal
Increase transparency and reduce uncertainty before policy signing.
Step 5A · Completion

E-Signature &
Confirmation

Goal
Complete the consultation flow through a fully digital experience.
Key Interactions
  • Reviewing documents digitally
  • Verifying customer information
  • Signing electronically on iPad
UX Goal
Create a seamless and trustworthy closing experience.
06 — Product Thinking Highlights

The Shift That Matters

From
displaying insurance products
→
To
simulating future possibilities
From
a premium calculator
→
To
a decision-support experience
From
an enterprise workflow
→
To
a human-centered financial consultation

The most significant transformation is not visual design itself, but the reframing of the product's role in financial decision-making.

07
07 Reflection

The Designer's Evolution

21
2021
Junior UI/UX Designer — visual execution, interface design
→
Transition
Gaining product thinking, system design, behavioral UX awareness
26
2026
Product Designer — systems, human behavior, business strategy

This project marked one of my earliest experiences working with enterprise products in the insurance industry.

Back in 2021, my primary focus was visual execution and interface design.

However, looking back years later, I realized that behind every screen existed an entire system involving operational workflows, business constraints, trust psychology, and financial decision-making.

This retrospective redesign also reflects my own professional evolution:

thinking about interfaces → thinking about products
thinking about pixels → thinking about systems
thinking about screens → thinking about human behavior

It represents an important milestone in my transition from UI Designer toward Product Designer.
